title: How caching works
text: Caching default. Enable construction point session: default cache directory system’s user cache folder R packages (tools::R_user_dir("ineptr2", "cache")). can override :
code: # At construction ine <- INEClient$new(use_cache = TRUE)  # Or later ine$use_cache <- TRUE ine <- INEClient$new(use_cache = TRUE, cache_dir = "my_cache")

title: How caching works
text: cache three multiple layers, serving different purpose: Every cached file tagged indicator code language, switching ine$lang "PT" "EN" maintains separate caches.

title: How caching works
text: downloading data, API response split chunks (one per HTTP request). chunk saved individual JSON file inside directory: manifest JSON file tracks download state: complete flag set true every chunk downloaded validated. mechanism enables resume support.
code: my_cache/   ine_0008273_EN_chunks/     chunk_0001.json     chunk_0002.json     chunk_0003.json   ine_0008273_EN_manifest.json {   "indicator": "0008273",   "lang": "EN",   "total_chunks": 3,   "urls": ["https://...chunk1", "https://...chunk2", "https://...chunk3"],   "complete": false }

title: How caching works
text: download interrupted — network timeout, session crash, simply close R — manifest completed chunks remain disk. call download_data() , ineptr2: Reads existing manifest Checks chunks already cached valid (non-empty, valid JSON) Skips downloads remaining chunks chunk first written temporary .part file renamed final .json name JSON validated. prevents half-written files corrupting cache.
code: # Session 1: starts downloading, gets interrupted at chunk 40 of 120 ine$download_data("0008206")  # Session 2 (later): resumes from chunk 41 ine$download_data("0008206") #> Resuming download: 40/120 chunks cached

title: How caching works
text: chunks downloaded assembled, get_data() processes tidy data frame caches result .rds file. cache also stores dimension filters used. subsequent calls, cache reused new request equal subset previously cached. example: avoids problem silently returning incomplete data filters change.
code: # First call: fetches and caches data for three regions ine$get_data("0008273", dim2 = c("11", "15", "17"))  # Second call: served from cache (equal to the cached filters) ine$get_data("0008273", dim2 = c("11", "15", "17"))  # Third call: served from cache (subset of the cached filters) ine$get_data("0008273", dim2 = c("11", "17"))  # Fourth call: cache miss — "20" was not in the original request ine$get_data("0008273", dim2 = c("11", "20"))

title: How caching works
text: chunk cache automatically invalidated dimension filters change downloads. ineptr2 detects comparing API URLs manifest URLs generated new request — different filters produce different URLs. general rule, 1) unsure dimensions may need 2) disk space issue, ’s better download full indicator, work cache. can also manually clear cache:
code: # Downloads with one set of filters ine$download_data("0008273", dim2 = c("11", "15"))  # Different filters: chunk cache is cleared and download starts from scratch ine$download_data("0008273", dim2 = c("11", "15", "17")) #> Dimension filters changed. Clearing chunk cache. # Clear cache for one indicator ine$clear_cache("0008273")  # Clear everything ine$clear_cache()

title: How caching works
text: Use list_cached() see ’s currently stored: useful check state partial downloads decide clear.
code: ine$list_cached() #>   indicator has_metadata has_data chunks_downloaded chunks_total download_complete #> 1   0008273         TRUE     TRUE                 3            3              TRUE #> 2   0008206         TRUE    FALSE                40          120             FALSE

title: How caching works
text: download_data() always writes file cache, even use_cache = FALSE. design — purpose populate cache later use via load_raw_data(): get_data() respects use_cache setting: enabled, checks processed data cache fetching; disabled, always hits API.
code: ine <- INEClient$new()  # use_cache defaults to FALSE  # Downloads to cache without loading into memory ine$download_data("0008206")  # Later: load the raw cached data raw <- ine$load_raw_data("0008206")

title: Getting started
text: INEClient object holds configuration — language, caching preferences, timeouts — don’t repeat every call. can change settings time:
code: ine <- INEClient$new(lang = "EN") ine #> <INEClient> #>   Language:          EN #>   Cache:             disabled #>   Row limit:         1,000,000 #>   Max retries:       3 #>   Progress interval: 10 #>   Timeout (s):       300 ine$lang <- "PT" ine$lang <- "EN"

title: Getting started
text: Suppose want work indicator "0008273" (Resident population). Start checking contains: shows indicator name, periodicity, time range, last update date, breakdown dimensions sizes. detail dimension, use get_dim_info(): see actual values available dimension: categ_cod column contains codes pass get_data() dimension filters.
code:
ine$info("0008273") #> #> Code: 0008273 #> Name: Resident population (No.) by Place of residence (NUTS - 2013), Sex and Age group; Annual - Statistics Portugal, Annual estimates of resident population #> Periodicity: Annual (2011 - 2023) #> Last updated: 2024-06-18 #> Dimensions: 4 #> dim1: Data reference period (13 values) #> dim2: Place of residence (NUTS - 2013) (344 values) #> dim3: Sex (3 values) #> dim4: Age group (19 values) ine$get_dim_info("0008273") #> dim_num abrv versao #> 1 1 Data reference period XXXXX #> 2 2 Place of residence (NUTS - 2013) 03505 #> 3 3 Sex 00305 #> 4 4 Age group 00708 #> nota_dsg #> 1 2011-2020, Final Resident Population Estimates - revised intercensal estimates (general regular revision) taking as reference the results of the 2011 and 2021 Censuses. From 2021 onwards, Provisional Resident Population Estimates - postcensal estimates based on the results of the 2021 Census .
2022, Provisional Resident Population Estimates revised in June 2024, which include in the resident population displaced persons from Ukraine who are beneficiaries of the Temporary Protection regime in Portugal. #> 2 Resident Population Estimates according to the administrative territorial division corresponding to the version of the Official Administrative Map of Portugal in force at the date of the 2021 Census (CAOP 2020) and the version of NUTS (NUTS 2013) in force from January 1, 2015. #> 3 #> 4 # All dimensions vals <- ine$get_dim_values("0008273") head(vals) #> dim_num cat_id categ_cod categ_dsg categ_ord categ_nivel #> Dim_Num1_S7A2011 1 S7A2011 S7A2011 2011 20110101 1 #> Dim_Num1_S7A2012 1 S7A2012 S7A2012 2012 20120101 1 #> Dim_Num1_S7A2013 1 S7A2013 S7A2013 2013 20130101 1 #> Dim_Num1_S7A2014 1 S7A2014 S7A2014 2014 20140101 1 #> Dim_Num1_S7A2015 1 S7A2015 S7A2015 2015 20150101 1 #> Dim_Num1_S7A2016 1 S7A2016 S7A2016 2016 20160101 1 # Only dimensions 1 and 3 ine$get_dim_values("0008273", dims = c(1, 3)) #> dim_num cat_id categ_cod categ_dsg categ_ord categ_nivel #> Dim_Num1_S7A2011 1 S7A2011 S7A2011 2011 20110101 1 #> Dim_Num1_S7A2012 1 S7A2012 S7A2012 2012 20120101 1 #> Dim_Num1_S7A2013 1 S7A2013 S7A2013 2013 20130101 1 #> Dim_Num1_S7A2014 1 S7A2014 S7A2014 2014 20140101 1 #> Dim_Num1_S7A2015 1 S7A2015 S7A2015 2015 20150101 1 #> Dim_Num1_S7A2016 1 S7A2016 S7A2016 2016 20160101 1 #> Dim_Num1_S7A2017 1 S7A2017 S7A2017 2017 20170101 1 #> Dim_Num1_S7A2018 1 S7A2018 S7A2018 2018 20180101 1 #> Dim_Num1_S7A2019 1 S7A2019 S7A2019 2019 20190101 1 #> Dim_Num1_S7A2020 1 S7A2020 S7A2020 2020 20200101 1 #> Dim_Num1_S7A2021 1 S7A2021 S7A2021 2021 20210101 1 #> Dim_Num1_S7A2022 1 S7A2022 S7A2022 2022 20220101 1 #> Dim_Num1_S7A2023 1 S7A2023 S7A2023 2023 20230101 1 #> Dim_Num3_T 3 T T MF 1 1 #> Dim_Num3_1 3 1 1 M 2 2 #> Dim_Num3_2 3 2 2 F 3 2

title: Getting started
text: fetching data, can check many API requests needed. 1 API request = 1 chunk: especially useful large indicators. can also preview filters see reduce number chunks:
code: ine$preview_chunks("0008206") #> Indicator '0008206' would require 66 chunks (estimated 63,293,076 rows) ine$preview_chunks("0008206", dim1 = "S7A2023") #> Indicator '0008206' would require 3 chunks (estimated 1,471,932 rows)

title: Getting started
text: simplest way get data get_data(), downloads processes one step: Dimension filters use categ_cod values get_dim_values(). omit dimension, values returned.
code: df <- ine$get_data("0008273", dim1 = "S7A2023", dim2 = c("PT", "1", "2", "3")) head(df) #>   dim_1 geocod                     geodsg dim_3 dim_3_t dim_4           dim_4_t #> 1  2023      2 Região Autónoma dos Açores     1       M    92 85 and more years #> 2  2023      3 Região Autónoma da Madeira     1       M    92 85 and more years #> 3  2023      2 Região Autónoma dos Açores     1       M    91     80 - 84 years #> 4  2023      3 Região Autónoma da Madeira     1       M    91     80 - 84 years #> 5  2023      2 Região Autónoma dos Açores     2       F    91     80 - 84 years #> 6  2023      2 Região Autónoma dos Açores     1       M    82     75 - 79 years #>   ind_string valor #> 1      1 361  1361 #> 2      1 731  1731 #> 3      1 911  1911 #> 4      2 441  2441 #> 5      3 034  3034 #> 6      3 299  3299

title: Getting started
text: large indicators, may prefer download first load later. streams data disk without holding memory: download interrupted, calling download_data() resumes left . Load raw data later (even new session):
code: ine$use_cache <- TRUE ine$cache_dir <- "C:/R/my_cache_dir" ine$download_data("0008273", dim1 = "S7A2023") #> Metadata cached at: C:/R/my_cache_dir/ine_0008273_EN_meta.json #> Downloaded chunk 1/1 #> All 1 chunks downloaded and cached #> Data downloaded to: /home/runner/work/ineptr2/ineptr2/vignettes/C:/R/my_cache_dir/ine_0008273_EN_chunks raw <- ine$load_raw_data("0008273") str(raw, max.level = 2) #> List of 2 #>  $ responses:List of 1 #>   ..$ :List of 1 #>  $ urls     : chr "https://www.ine.pt/ine/json_indicador/pindica.jsp?op=2&varcd=0008273&lang=EN&Dim1=S7A2023"

title: Getting started
text: default, every call hits API. Enable caching avoid redundant requests: caching , get_data() stores processed result. Subsequent calls narrower filters served cache. See caching works article details.

title: Getting started
text: ’ve previously downloaded data want know whether indicator updated since: Assuming cached metadata indicator 0008206, field DataUltimaAtualizacao used. Alternatively, provide specific last_updated date.
code: ine$is_updated("0008273", metadata = ine$get_metadata("0008273")) #> Using cached metadata #> [1] FALSE # OR ine$is_updated("0008273", last_updated = "2024-01-01") #> [1] TRUE

title: Getting started
text: sure indicator code exists?
code: ine$is_valid("0008273") #> Using cached metadata #> [1] TRUE ine$is_valid("0000000") #> Metadata cached at: /home/runner/work/ineptr2/ineptr2/vignettes/C:/R/my_cache_dir/ine_0000000_EN_meta.json #> [1] FALSE

title: Getting started
text: sure indicator need? can download full INE catalog — table 13,000+ indicators, codes, names, themes: catalog download slow (~10 minutes) cached disk, subsequent calls return instantly.

title: Getting started
text: output, including dimension labels descriptions, follows client’s language setting:
code: ine_pt <- INEClient$new(lang = "PT") ine_pt$info("0008273") #> <INE Indicator> #>   Código: 0008273 #>   Nome: População residente (N.º) por Local de residência (NUTS - 2013), Sexo e Grupo etário; Anual - INE, Estimativas anuais da população residente #>   Periodicidade: Anual (2011 - 2023) #>   Últ. atualização: 2024-06-18 #>   Dimensões: 4 #>     dim1: Período de referência dos dados (13 valores) #>     dim2: Local de residência (NUTS - 2013) (344 valores) #>     dim3: Sexo (3 valores) #>     dim4: Grupo etário (19 valores)

title: Getting started
text: longer need indicator? Clean cache way .
code: # cleanup - single indicator ine$clear_cache("0008273") #> Cache cleared for indicator: 0008273  # everything # WARNING: This will remove `cache_dir` and all its contents. ine$clear_cache() #> Cache cleared: /home/runner/work/ineptr2/ineptr2/vignettes/C:/R/my_cache_dir

title: What changed from ineptR
text: ineptr2 targets version 2 INE API, introduced richer dimension filtering remaining backward-compatible V1 queries. key additions: Dim1=T (periods) — setting dimension 1 value T retrieves available reference periods, instead perform one call period explicitly. Comma-separated values — multiple values single dimension parameter (e.g.Dim1=S7A2019,S7A2020), enabling multi-value queries without multiple API calls. Level-based selection (lvl@N) — retrieve entries given hierarchical level (e.g. lvl@5 geographic dimension returns municipalities). Classification subtree (<*>CODE) — retrieve children classification node (e.g. <*>200 geographic dimension returns NUTS III - Madeira region municipalities). 1 million rows - limit greatly increased 40 000 1 000 000 returned rows per call. features let ineptr2 build smarter, compact URL grids — directly reduces number HTTP requests needed filtered queries. example, full 0008206 indicator now requires 66 calls, compared 2000 calls V1.

what: R6 client instead of standalone functions
title: What changed from ineptR
text: ineptR exported five standalone functions (get_ine_data(), get_metadata(), get_dim_info(), get_dim_values(), is_indicator_valid()). Every call stateless — pass lang time. Caching implemented: ineptr2 wraps everything single R6 object. Configure , call methods: now concise (now: get_data; : get_ine_data) coherent (now: get_data, get_metadata; : get_ine_data, get_metadata). Settings live — change ine$lang mid-session every subsequent call picks , validation built (e.g. lang accepts "PT" "EN", row_limit capped API maximum).
code: # ineptR — repeat lang on every call df <- get_ine_data("0008273", lang = "EN") meta <- get_metadata("0008273", lang = "EN") dims <- get_dim_values("0008273", lang = "EN") # ineptr2 — configure once, use everywhere ine <- INEine$new(lang = "EN") df <- ine$get_data("0008273") meta <- ine$get_metadata("0008273") dims <- ine$get_dim_values("0008273")

title: What changed from ineptR
text: ineptR pulled 11 packages install time: dplyr, httr, httr2, lifecycle, magrittr, progressr, purrr, readr, stringr, tibble, tidyr ineptr2 needs 4 packages: httr2, jsonlite, R6, xml2 data wrangling now uses base R entire tidyverse chain gone. package installs faster smaller footprint.

title: What changed from ineptR
text: INE API limits response maximum number rows. ineptR implemented maximum 40 000 rows per request (max_cells), meant large indicators required hundreds thousands small API calls. ineptr2 defaults much higher limit (row_limit = 1 000 000) splitting logic improved (Dimension 1 now accepts multiple values per call). result far fewer HTTP requests data. can preview chunk plan committing download:

title: What changed from ineptR
text: ineptR caching — every call hit API fresh, data lived memory. downloading large indicators, user responsible cycle get data - save disk - get data. ineptr2 introduces chunk-based file cache three layers: Chunk cache — raw API responses stored individual JSON files disk Data cache — processed data stored RDS, dimension filters tracked cache reused new request subset cached Metadata cache — used is_updated() detect changes without full download Caching enabled via ine$use_cache <- TRUE construction time, cache directory configurable. See caching works article detailed walkthrough.

what: Download and load as separate steps
title: What changed from ineptR
text: ineptR always downloaded processed data one go via get_ine_data(). large indicators meant holding everything memory risking --emory errors. ineptr2 separates two concerns: means can download overnight, close R, load results later.

title: What changed from ineptR
text: download interrupted (network drop, session crash, timeout), ineptr2 picks left . download creates JSON manifest tracking chunks succeeded. Calling download_data() skips completed chunks automatically.

title: What changed from ineptR
text: ineptr2 exposes methods download (ine$download_catalog()) load (ine$get_catalog()) full indicator catalog 13 000 indicators.

title: What changed from ineptR
text: ineptr2 adds several methods equivalent ineptR: ine$info() — prints human-readable summary indicator: name, periodicity, time range, dimensions sizes. ine$is_updated() — compares cached metadata live API check data updated since last download. ine$preview_chunks() — shows many API calls download require, commit . ine$get_catalog() / ine$download_catalog() — retrieve full INE indicator catalog. ine$list_cached() — lists indicators currently file cache. ine$clear_cache() — clears cached files one indicators.

text: Matos C (2026). ineptr2: Wrapper Statistics Portugal API. R package version 0.3.0, https://c-matos.github.io/ineptr2/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {ineptr2: Wrapper for Statistics Portugal API},   author = {Carlos Matos},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.3.0},   url = {https://c-matos.github.io/ineptr2/}, }

title: Wrapper for Statistics Portugal API
text: ineptr2 R client Statistics Portugal (INE) API. INE publishes 13 000 statistical indicators covering demographics, health, economy, education, environment . API provides programmatic access . Working API directly can cumbersome: Responses capped 1 million returned rows per request. Multiple requests may required larger indicators. Indicator structure (dimensions, codes, time periods) must previously known explored separate metadata endpoints can build specific query. built-way resume interrupted downloads avoid redundant requests. ineptr2 handles single R6 client: Automatic chunking - splits large requests reassembles results transparently. user needs perform one function call. File-based caching - stores raw chunks, processed data, metadata disk resume support. large indicator breaks mid-download, resume later break point. Exploration helpers - quality life methods inspect dimensions, preview download size, check updates, validate indicator codes fetching data. package targets version 2 INE API, extended functionality previous verison multiple time values per request, level-based selection, much larger response size (40k 1M rows).

title: Wrapper for Statistics Portugal API
code: # From CRAN # install.packages("ineptr2") # Currently waiting publication on CRAN  # Development version from GitHub # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("c-matos/ineptr2")

title: Wrapper for Statistics Portugal API
text: larger indicators, can filter dimension get need: Use ine$get_dim_values() discover valid codes dimension.
code: library(ineptr2)  ine <- INEClient$new()  # Fetch data for a small indicator df <- ine$get_data("0010003") head(df) #>   dim_1 geocod   geodsg ind_string valor #> 1  2010     PT Portugal      58,31 58.31 #> 2  2011     PT Portugal      60,04 60.04 #> 3  2012     PT Portugal      60,53 60.53 #> 4  2013     PT Portugal      58,92 58.92 #> 5  2014     PT Portugal      62,10  62.1 #> 6  2015     PT Portugal      61,28 61.28 # Resident population, year 2023, NUTS 1 regions only df <- ine$get_data(   "0008273",   dim1 = "S7A2023",   dim2 = c("PT", "1", "2", "3"),   dim3 = "T" ) head(df) #>   dim_1 geocod                     geodsg dim_3 dim_3_t dim_4      dim_4_t #> 1  2023      2 Região Autónoma dos Açores     T      HM    81 70 - 74 anos #> 2  2023      1                 Continente     T      HM    81 70 - 74 anos #> 3  2023      3 Região Autónoma da Madeira     T      HM    81 70 - 74 anos #> 4  2023     PT                   Portugal     T      HM    81 70 - 74 anos #> 5  2023      2 Região Autónoma dos Açores     T      HM    82 75 - 79 anos #> 6  2023      1                 Continente     T      HM    82 75 - 79 anos #>   ind_string  valor #> 1     11 003  11003 #> 2    594 627 594627 #> 3     13 513  13513 #> 4    619 143 619143 #> 5      7 893   7893 #> 6    498 889 498889

title: Wrapper for Statistics Portugal API
text: Get started — full walkthrough: creating client, exploring indicators, downloading data, caching. caching works — chunk cache, manifests, resume support, cache invalidation. changed ineptR — differences previous version.

title: INEClient configuration fields — INEClient-fields
text: Configuration fields INEClient class. fields implemented active bindings validation. Set ine$field <- value read ine$field.

title: INEClient configuration fields — INEClient-fields
text: lang Character. Language code: "PT" (default) "EN". Affects API responses, cache file paths, display labels. use_cache Logical. Whether cache API responses locally. Default FALSE. cache_dir Character NULL. Cache directory path. NULL (default), uses tools::R_user_dir("ineptr2", "cache"). row_limit Integer. Maximum output rows per API request splitting chunks. Must 1 1 000 000 (API ceiling). Default 1000000L. max_retries Integer. Maximum retry attempts failed chunk downloads. Default 3L. progress_interval Integer. Print progress message every N chunks downloads. Default 10L. timeout Numeric. Timeout seconds API requests (metadata data endpoints). Default 300 (5 minutes). catalog endpoint uses separate, longer timeout.

title: INEClient configuration fields — INEClient-fields
code: ine <- INEClient$new() ine$lang #> [1] "PT" ine$lang <- "EN"  ine$use_cache <- TRUE ine$cache_dir <- tempdir() ine$row_limit <- 500000L

title: INE API Client — INEClient
text: R6 class providing access Statistics Portugal (INE) API. Holds configuration state (language, caching preferences) provides methods retrieving data, metadata, indicator catalog. See INEClient-fields configurable fields (language, caching, timeouts, etc.).

title: INE API Client — INEClient
text: get_data(indicator, row_limit, ...) Retrieve tidy data indicator, automatic chunking optional caching. download_data(indicator, row_limit, ...) Download data file cache without loading memory. load_raw_data(indicator) Load previously downloaded raw JSON data file cache. preview_chunks(indicator, row_limit, ...) Preview many API chunks download require.

title: INE API Client — INEClient
text: get_metadata(indicator) Get cleaned metadata indicator. info(indicator) Print summary indicator's key properties. get_dim_info(indicator) Get dimension descriptions. get_dim_values(indicator, dims) Get possible values dimensions. is_valid(indicator) Check indicator exists. is_updated(indicator, last_updated, metadata) Check indicator updated since last download.

title: INE API Client — INEClient
text: get_catalog() Download parse full indicator catalog (~10 min). download_catalog() Download catalog file cache.

title: INE API Client — INEClient
text: list_cached() List indicators present file cache. clear_cache(indicator) Clear cached files.

title: INE API Client — INEClient
text: lang Language code ("PT" "EN"). use_cache Whether caching enabled. cache_dir Cache directory path, NULL default. row_limit Default maximum output rows per API request. max_retries Maximum retry attempts chunk downloads. progress_interval Print progress every N chunks downloads. timeout Timeout seconds API requests.

title: INE API Client — INEClient
text: INEClient$new() INEClient$get_data() INEClient$download_data() INEClient$load_raw_data() INEClient$get_metadata() INEClient$get_catalog() INEClient$download_catalog() INEClient$info() INEClient$get_dim_info() INEClient$get_dim_values() INEClient$preview_chunks() INEClient$is_valid() INEClient$is_updated() INEClient$list_cached() INEClient$clear_cache() INEClient$print() INEClient$clone()

title: INE API Client — INEClient
text: Create new INE API client.

title: INE API Client — INEClient
code: INEClient$new(   lang = "PT",   use_cache = FALSE,   cache_dir = NULL,   row_limit = 1000000L,   max_retries = 3L,   progress_interval = 10L,   timeout = 300 )

title: INE API Client — INEClient
text: lang Language code: "PT" (default) "EN". use_cache Logical. Whether cache API responses. Default FALSE. cache_dir Character NULL. Cache directory path. NULL (default), uses tools::R_user_dir("ineptr2", "cache"). row_limit Integer. Default maximum output rows per API request. Default 1000000L. max_retries Integer. Maximum retry attempts failed chunk downloads. Default 3L. progress_interval Integer. Print progress message every N chunks downloads. Default 10L. timeout Numeric. Timeout seconds API requests (metadata data endpoints). Default 300 (5 minutes). catalog endpoint uses separate, longer timeout.

title: INE API Client — INEClient

title: INE API Client — INEClient
text: Retrieve tidy data indicator.

title: INE API Client — INEClient
text: indicator INE indicator ID 7-digit string. Example: "0010003". row_limit Integer NULL. Maximum output rows per API request splitting multiple calls. NULL (default), uses client's row_limit field. See Details. ... Dimension filters. argument named dimN (N dimension number) character vector values. Omitted dimensions include values.

title: INE API Client — INEClient
text: INE API limits request 1 000 000 output rows, counted product unique values across dimensions. estimated row count exceeds row_limit, request automatically split smaller chunks iterating one dimensions. requests timing , try lowering row_limit (increasing client's timeout field) produce , smaller chunks.

title: INE API Client — INEClient
text: use_cache enabled, processed data stored RDS file. Subsequent calls narrower dimension filters return cached result without hitting API. Changing filters include values outside cached set triggers fresh download.

text: indicator INE indicator ID 7-digit string. Example: "0010003". row_limit Integer NULL. Maximum output rows per API request splitting multiple calls. NULL (default), uses client's row_limit field. ... Dimension filters form dimN = value.

title: INE API Client — INEClient
code: # -- Setup -- ine <- INEClient$new() ine <- INEClient$new(lang = "EN", use_cache = TRUE) print(ine) #> <INEClient> #>   Language:          EN #>   Cache:             enabled (/home/runner/.cache/R/ineptr2) #>   Row limit:         1,000,000 #>   Max retries:       3 #>   Progress interval: 10 #>   Timeout (s):       300  # -- Metadata -- meta <- ine$get_metadata("0010003") #> Metadata cached at: /home/runner/.cache/R/ineptr2/ine_0010003_EN_meta.json ine$info("0010003") #> Using cached metadata #> <INE Indicator> #>   Code: 0010003 #>   Name: Proportion of domestic budget funded by domestic taxes (%); Annual - Budget Directorate-General (Ministry of Finance) #>   Periodicity: Annual (2010 - 2026) #>   Last updated: 2026-01-30 #>   Dimensions: 2 #>     dim1: Data reference period (17 values) #>     dim2: Geographic localization (Portugal) (1 values) dims <- ine$get_dim_info("0010003") #> Using cached metadata vals <- ine$get_dim_values("0010003") #> Using cached metadata  # -- Data -- df <- ine$get_data("0010003") #> Using cached metadata #> Downloaded chunk 1/1 #> All 1 chunks downloaded and cached df <- ine$get_data("0010003", dim1 = "S7A2024", dim2 = c("11", "17")) #> Using cached metadata #> Using cached processed data ine$preview_chunks("0008273") #> Metadata cached at: /home/runner/.cache/R/ineptr2/ine_0008273_EN_meta.json #> Indicator '0008273' would require 1 chunk (estimated 254,904 rows)  # -- Validation -- ine$is_valid("0010003") #> Using cached metadata #> [1] TRUE ine$is_updated("0010003", last_updated = "2024-01-01") #> [1] TRUE  # -- Cache -- ine$list_cached() #>   indicator has_metadata has_data chunks_downloaded chunks_total #> 1   0008273         TRUE    FALSE                NA           NA #> 2   0010003         TRUE     TRUE                 1            1 #>   download_complete #> 1             FALSE #> 2              TRUE ine$clear_cache() #> Cache cleared: /home/runner/.cache/R/ineptr2
